Installation

Merlin runs as a standard Laravel application on your own server or local machine. The quick-install path handles everything in one command. The manual path is provided for environments where you need more control over each step.

Requirements

  • PHP 8.4 with the extensions Laravel requires (mbstring, pdo, openssl, tokenizer, xml, ctype, json, bcmath, fileinfo)
  • Composer (PHP dependency manager)
  • Node.js and npm — NEEDS CONFIRMATION: minimum Node version
  • MariaDB (preferred) or MySQL for the database
  • Magika — a command-line binary used for file-type detection during invoice processing; must be installed and accessible on the system path, or its path set in MAGIKA_BINARY
  • Anthropic API key — required for AI invoice extraction; obtain one from the Anthropic console

Quick install

The setup Composer script handles the full installation sequence in one command:

git clone https://github.com/nettsite/merlin.git
cd merlin
composer run setup

The setup script runs these steps in order: composer install, copy .env.example to .env if it does not already exist, php artisan key:generate, php artisan migrate --force, npm install, and npm run build.

After it completes, open .env and fill in the database credentials and API keys described in Configuration, then run the seeders described in Seeding Reference Data.

Manual installation

If you prefer to run each step yourself, follow the sections below in order.

Clone and install dependencies

  1. Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/nettsite/merlin.git && cd merlin
  2. Install PHP dependencies: composer install
  3. Install Node dependencies: npm install

Environment file

  1. Copy the example environment file: cp .env.example .env
  2. Generate the application key: php artisan key:generate
  3. Open .env and fill in your database connection, Anthropic API key, and any other variables. See Configuration for the full list.

Database

  1. Create a database and set DB_DATABASE, DB_USERNAME, and DB_PASSWORD in .env.
  2. Run migrations: php artisan migrate

Migrations do not have rollback methods — they are forward-only. Do not run migrate:rollback against a database that holds data you want to keep.

Build front-end assets

Merlin uses Vite to bundle its CSS and JavaScript. You must build the assets before opening the application in a browser:

npm run build

If the build step is skipped, the application will return a Vite manifest not found error on every page. See Troubleshooting — Vite manifest errors if you encounter this.

Creating your first user

Run the default admin user seeder to create an initial Administrator account:

php artisan db:seed --class=DefaultAdminUserSeeder

This creates a user with the email admin@example.com and the password changeme, and assigns it the Administrator role. Change the password immediately after first login. Do not use these credentials in a production environment.

You also need to run the remaining seeders to create the default chart of accounts, roles, and payment terms before the application is fully usable. See Seeding Reference Data.

Running the development server

The dev Composer script starts all four development processes concurrently:

composer run dev

This launches the PHP development server, a queue worker, log streaming (Pail), and the Vite development server in a single terminal session. All processes are restarted together when you press Ctrl+C.

To run the test suite:

php artisan test --compact
